name: lazyslide

metadata.name: lazyslide
metadata.summary: Modularized and scalable whole slide image analysis

documentation:
# LazySlide

Modularized and scalable whole slide image analysis

[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/lazyslide/badge/?version=stable&style=flat-square)](https://lazyslide.readthedocs.io/en/stable) ![pypi version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/lazyslide?color=0098FF&logo=python&logoColor=white&style=flat-square) ![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/lazyslide?color=FFD43B&style=flat-square) LazySlide is a Python package for whole-slide image (WSI) processing. It is designed to be fast and memory-efficient, allowing users to work with large WSIs on modest hardware. ## Highlights - Multimodel analysis - Transcriptomics integration - `scanpy`-style API - CLI and Nextflow support

functions.name: lazyslide._tools._features.feature_extraction
documentation:
Extract features from WSI tiles using a pre-trained model.

# Parameters

wsi

: The whole-slide image object.

model

: The model used for image feature extraction.
  Built-in foundation models include:

  - 'uni': UNI model from Mahmood Lab.
  - 'conch': CONCH model from Mahmood Lab for text-image co-embedding.
  - 'conch_vision': CONCH model for only vision task.
  - 'gigapath': GigaPath model from Microsoft.
  - 'plip': PLIP model from Standford Zou Lab for text-image co-embedding.
  - 'plip_vision': PLIP model for only vision task.

  Other models can be loaded from torchvision:
  See <https://pytorch.org/vision/stable/models.html> for available models.
  Here list some commonly used models in digital pathology:

  - 'resnet50'
  - 'vgg16'
  - 'convnet_base'

model_path

: The path to the model file. Either model or model_path must be provided.
  If you don't have internet access, you can download the model file and load it from the local path.
  You can also load custom models from local files.

token

: The token for downloading the model from Hugging Face Hub for foundation models.

load_kws

: Options to pass to the model creation function.

transform

: The transform function for the input image.
  If not provided, a default ImageNet transform function will be used.

compile

: Whether to compile the model for faster inference.

compile_kws

: Options to pass to the {class}`torch.compile` function.

device

: The device to use for inference. If not provided, the device will be automatically selected.

tile_key

: The key of the tiles dataframe in the spatial data object.

key_added

: The key to store the extracted features.

batch_size

: The batch size for inference.

num_workers
: - mode='batch', The number of workers for data loading.
  - mode='chunk', The number of workers for parallel inference.

mode
: - 'batch': The data loader will load the data in batches. Only one model instance is launched.
  - 'chunk': Multiple model instances are launched for parallel inference. This mode is only available for CPU.

pbar

: Whether to show progress bar.

return_features

: Whether to return the extracted features.

# Returns

None or ndarray

: If return_features is True, return the extracted features.

- The feature matrix will be added to {bdg-danger}`tables` slot of the spatial data object.
TODO: Test if it's possible to load model files

functions.name: lazyslide._tools._features.feature_aggregation
documentation:
Feature aggregation on different levels.

# Parameters

wsi

: The whole-slide image object.

feature_key

: The key to indicate which feature to aggregate.

layer_key

: The key of the layer in the feature table.

encoder
: - Numpy functions: 'mean', 'median', 'sum', 'std', 'var', ...
  - 'gigapath': GigaPath slide encoder. The feature must be extracted by GigaPath model.

tile_key

: The key of the tiles dataframe in the spatial data object.

by

: The level to aggregate the features.
  \- 'slide': Aggregate the features from all tiles in the slide.
  \- Column name in tile dataframe: Aggregate the features by specific column.

  > For example, to aggregate by tissue pieces, set by='tissue_id'.

agg_key

: The key to store the aggregated features. If not provided, the key will be 'agg\_\{by}'.

# Returns

The aggregated features will be added to the {bdg-danger}`varm` slot of the feature {code}`AnnData`.
The aggregation operation will be recorded in the {bdg-danger}`uns` slot.

functions.name: lazyslide._preprocess._tissue.find_tissues
documentation:
Find tissue regions in the WSI and add them as contours and holes.

# Parameters

wsi

: The WSIData object to work on.

level

: The level to use for segmentation.

use_saturation

: The tissue image will be converted from RGB to HSV space,
  the saturation channel (color purity) will be used for tissue detection.

blur_ksize

: The kernel size used to apply median blurring.

threshold

: The threshold for binary thresholding.

morph_n_iter

: The number of iterations of morphological opening and closing to apply.

morph_k_size

: The kernel size for morphological opening and closing.

min_tissue_area

: The minimum area of tissue.

min_hole_area

: The minimum area of holes.

detect_holes

: Detect holes in tissue regions.

filter_artifacts

: Filter artifacts out. Artifacts that are non-redish are removed.

key_added

: The key to save the result in the WSIData object.

# Returns

- A {class}`GeoDataFrame <geopandas.GeoDataFrame>` with columns of 'tissue_id' and 'geometry'.
  added to the {bdg-danger}`shapes` slot of the SpatialData object.

# Examples

```
.. plot::
    :context: close-figs

    >>> from wsidata import open_wsi
    >>> import lazyslide as zs
    >>> wsi = open_wsi("https://github.com/camicroscope/Distro/raw/master/images/sample.svs")
    >>> zs.pp.find_tissues(wsi)
    >>> zs.pl.tissue(wsi)
```
TODO: TO have a better hole detection
We can first identify the tissue regions at the least resolution level
Then we get the bbox of the tissue regions, and then we can go to higher resolution levels

functions.name: lazyslide._preprocess._tissue.tissues_qc
documentation:
Score tissue regions in the WSI for QC

This is useful to filter out artifacts or non-tissue regions.

# Parameters

wsi

: The WSIData object to work on.

scores

: {class}`Scorer` to use for scoring tissue regions.
  \- 'redness': The redness of the tissue.
  \- 'brightness': The brightness of the tissue.

num_workers

: Number of workers to use for scoring.

pbar

: Show progress bar.

tissue_key

: Key of the tissue data in the {bdg-danger}`shapes` slot.

qc_key

: The key in the tissue dataframe indicates if a tissue passed qc.

# Examples

```
>>> from wsidata import open_wsi
>>> import lazyslide as zs
>>> wsi = open_wsi("https://github.com/camicroscope/Distro/raw/master/images/sample.svs")
>>> zs.pp.find_tissues(wsi)
>>> zs.pp.tissues_qc(wsi, ["redness", "brightness"])
>>> wsi["tissues"]
```

variables.name: lazyslide._preprocess._tissue.TARGET
documentation: mpp = 0.5 and downsample = 4

functions.name: lazyslide._preprocess._tiles.tile_tissues
documentation:
Generate tiles within the tissue contours in the WSI.

# Parameters

wsi

: The WSIData object to work on.

tile_px

: The size of the tile, if tuple, (W, H).

stride_px

: The stride of the tile, if tuple, (W, H).
  If None, use the tile size.

edge

: Whether to include the edge tiles.

mpp

: The requested mpp of the tiles, if None, use the slide mpp.

slide_mpp

: This value will override the slide mpp.

tolerance

: The tolerable deviation when matching the mpp.
  If requested mpp does not match the naive mpp,
  resize operation is needed when generating the tiles.

method

: The flavor of the tile generation, either 'polygon-test' or 'mask':
  \- 'polygon-test': Use point polygon test to check if the tiles points are inside the contours.
  \- 'mask': Transform the contours and holes into binary mask and check the fraction of background.

background_fraction

: For {code}`method='mask'`,
  The fraction of background in the tile, if more than this, discard the tile.

min_pts

: For {code}`method='polygon-test'`,
  The minimum number of points of a rectangle tile that should be inside the tissue.
  Should be within {code}`[0, 4]`.

errors

: The error handling strategy, either 'raise' or 'warn'.

tissue_key

: The key of the tissue contours.

key_added

: The key of the tiles.

# Examples

```
.. plot::
    :context: close-figs

    >>> from wsidata import open_wsi
    >>> import lazyslide as zs
    >>> wsi = open_wsi("https://github.com/camicroscope/Distro/raw/master/images/sample.svs")
    >>> zs.pp.find_tissues(wsi)
    >>> zs.pp.tile_tissues(wsi, 256, mpp=0.5)
    >>> zs.pl.tiles(wsi, tissue_id=0, show_grid=True, show_point=False)
```

functions.name: lazyslide._preprocess._tiles.tiles_qc
documentation:
Score the tiles and filter the tiles based on their quality scores.

# Parameters

wsi

: The WSIData object to work on.

scorers

: The scorer object or a callable that takes in an image and returns a score.
  You can also pass in a string:
  \- 'focus': A FocusLite scorer that will score the focus of the image
  \- 'contrast': A Contrast scorer that will score the contrast of the image
  \- 'brightness': A Brightness scorer that will score the brightness of the image
  \- 'redness': A SplitRGB scorer that will score the redness of the image

num_workers

: The number of workers to use.

pbar

: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

tile_key

: The key of the tiles in the {bdg-danger}`shapes` slot.

qc_key

: The key in the tiles dataframe indicates if a tile passed qc.

# Returns

The columns with scores and the key added to the spatial data object.

# Examples

```python
>>> from wsidata import open_wsi
>>> import lazyslide as zs
>>> wsi = open_wsi("https://github.com/camicroscope/Distro/raw/master/images/sample.svs")
>>> zs.pp.find_tissues(wsi)
>>> zs.pp.tile_tissues(wsi, 256, mpp=0.5)
>>> zs.pp.tiles_qc(wsi, scorers=["focus", "contrast"])
>>> wsi['tiles'].head(n=2)
```

functions.name: lazyslide._preprocess._tiles.create_tiles
documentation:
Create the tiles, return coordination that comprise the tiles
    and the index of points for each rectangular.

Tips: The number of tiles has nothing to do with the tile size,
it's decided by stride size.

Parameters
----------
image_shape : (int, int)
    The (H, W) of the image.
tile_w, tile_h: int
    The width/height of tiles.
stride_w, stride_h : int, default None
    The width/height of stride when moving to the next tile.
edge : bool, default True
    Whether to include the edge tiles.

Returns
-------
coordinates : np.ndarray (N, 2)
    The coordinates of the tiles, N is the number of tiles.
indices : np.ndarray (M, 4)
    The indices of the points for each rect, M is the number of rects.

modules.name: lazyslide._cv.transform
documentation: This module is highly inspired by both torchvison and pathml

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.compose.TissueDetectionHE
documentation:
Detect tissue regions from H&E stained slide.
First applies a median blur, then binary thresholding, then morphological opening and closing, and finally
foreground detection.

Parameters
----------
use_saturation : bool
    Whether to convert to HSV and use saturation channel for tissue detection.
    If False, convert from RGB to greyscale and use greyscale image_ref for tissue detection. Defaults to True.
blur_ksize : int
    kernel size used to apply median blurring. Defaults to 15.
threshold : int
    threshold for binary thresholding. If None, uses Otsu's method. Defaults to None.
morph_n_iter : int
    number of iterations of morphological opening and closing to apply. Defaults to 3.
morph_k_size : int
    kernel size for morphological opening and closing. Defaults to 7.
min_region_size : int

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.compose.Mask2Polygon
documentation:
Convert binary mask to polygon.

Parameters
----------
min_area : int
    Minimum area of detected regions to be included in the polygon.

functions.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.utils.dtype_limits
documentation:
Return intensity limits, i.e. (min, max) tuple, of the image's dtype.

Parameters
----------
image : ndarray
    Input image.
clip_negative : bool, optional
    If True, clip the negative range (i.e. return 0 for min intensity)
    even if the image dtype allows negative values.

Returns
-------
imin, imax : tuple
    Lower and upper intensity limits.

Image Transform base class.

Image -> Image

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.MedianBlur
documentation:
Median blur kernel.

Parameters
----------
kernel_size : int
        Width of kernel. Must be an odd number. Defaults to 5.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.GaussianBlur
documentation:
Gaussian blur kernel.

Parameters
----------
kernel_size : int
        Width of kernel. Must be an odd number. Defaults to 5.
sigma : float
        Variance of Gaussian kernel. Variance is assumed to be equal in X and Y axes. Defaults to 5.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.BoxBlur
documentation:
Box (average) blur kernel.

Parameters
----------
kernel_size : int
    Width of kernel. Defaults to 5.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.BinaryThreshold
documentation:
Binary thresholding transform to create a binary mask.
If input image is RGB it is first converted to greyscale, otherwise the input must have 1 channel.

# Parameters

use_otsu

: Whether to use Otsu's method to automatically determine optimal threshold. Defaults to True.

threshold

: Specified threshold. Ignored if `use_otsu is True`. Defaults to 0.

inverse

: Whether to use inverse threshold. If using inverse threshold, pixels below the threshold will
  be returned as 1. Otherwise, pixels below the threshold will be returned as 0. Defaults to `False`.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.ArtifactFilterThreshold
documentation:
Artifact filter thresholding transform to create a binary mask.

Parameters
----------
threshold : int
    Threshold value for artifact filter.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.MorphOpen
documentation:
Morphological opening. First applies erosion operation, then dilation.
Reduces noise by removing small objects from the background.
Operates on a binary mask.

Parameters
----------
kernel_size : int
    Size of kernel for default square kernel. Ignored if a custom kernel is specified.
    Defaults to 5.
n_iterations : int
    Number of opening operations to perform. Defaults to 1.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.MorphClose
documentation:
Morphological closing. First applies dilation operation, then erosion.
Reduces noise by closing small holes in the foreground.
Operates on a binary mask.

Parameters
----------
kernel_size : int
    Size of kernel for default square kernel. Ignored if a custom kernel is specified.
    Defaults to 5.
n_iterations : int
    Number of opening operations to perform. Defaults to 1.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.transform.mods.ForegroundDetection
documentation:
Foreground detection for binary masks. Identifies regions that have a total area greater than
specified threshold. Supports including holes within foreground regions, or excluding holes
above a specified area threshold.

Parameters
----------
min_foreground_area : int or within (0, 1)
    If int, minimum area of detected foreground regions, in pixels area.
    If within (0, 1), minimum area of detected foreground regions, in proportion to the total image area.
min_hole_area : int or within (0, 1)
    If int, maximum size of allowed holes in foreground regions, in pixels area.
    If within (0, 1), maximum size of allowed holes in foreground regions, in proportion to the total image area.
detect_holes : bool
    Whether to detect holes in foreground regions. Defaults to True.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.base.ScorerBase

methods.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.base.ScorerBase.apply
documentation: The scorer will return the scores and the bool value indicating of QC

Base class for all scorers.

All scores are operated on a patch.

Image -> float

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.base.ComposeScorer
documentation:
Compose multiple scorers into one.

Parameters
----------
scorers : List[ScorerBase]
    List of scorers to be composed.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.module.SplitRGB
documentation:
Calculate the RGB value of a patch.

Brightness is calculated as the mean of the pixel values.

The patch need to be in shape (H, W, 3).

Parameters
----------
red_threshold : float
    Threshold to determine if a patch is red enough.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.module.Contrast
documentation:
Calculate the contrast of a patch.

Contrast is calculated as the standard deviation of the pixel values.

Parameters
----------
threshold : float
    Threshold to determine if a patch is contrasted or not.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.module.Sharpness
documentation:
Calculate the sharpness of a patch.

Sharpness is calculated as the variance of the Laplacian of the pixel values.

Parameters
----------
threshold : float
    Threshold to determine if a patch is sharp or not.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.module.Sobel
documentation:
Calculate the sobel of a patch.

Sobel is calculated as the variance of the Sobel of the pixel values.

Parameters
----------
threshold : float
    Threshold to determine if a patch is sharp or not.

classes.name: lazyslide._cv.scorer.module.Canny
documentation:
Calculate the canny of a patch.

Canny is calculated as the variance of the Canny of the pixel values.

Parameters
----------
threshold : float
    Threshold to determine if a patch is sharp or not.

functions.name: lazyslide._plotting._api.tissue
documentation:
Display the tissue image.

# Parameters

wsi

: The whole-slide image object.

tissue_id

: The tissue id (piece) to extract.

tissue_key

: The tissue key.

title

: The title of the plot.

show_contours

: Show the tissue contours.

show_origin

: Show the origin.

show_id

: Show the tissue id.

render_size

: The size of the rendered image.
  Increase this value for better image quality.

ax

: The axes to plot on.

# Examples

```
.. plot::
    :context: close-figs

    >>> import lazyslide as zs
    >>> wsi = zs.open_wsi("https://github.com/camicroscope/Distro/raw/master/images/sample.svs")
    >>> zs.pp.find_tissues(wsi)
    >>> zs.pl.tissue(wsi)
```

functions.name: lazyslide._plotting._api.tiles
documentation:
Display the tiles.

# Parameters

wsi

: The whole-slide image object.

feature_key

: The feature key assigned when generating the numeric tile features.

color

: The column/ feature name that should be visualized from feature_key.
  If feature_key is None, this is the column name from the tiles table.

tissue_id

: The tissue id (piece) to plot.
  If None, all will be plotted.

tissue_key

: The tissue key.

tile_key

: The tile key.

title

: The title of the plot.

show_tissue

: Show the tissue image.

show_point

: Show the points.
  By default, the points are black.

show_grid

: Show the tiles grid.

show_contours

: Show the tissue contours.

show_origin

: Show the origin.

show_id

: Show the tissue (piece) id.

render_size

: The size of the rendered image.

alpha

: The transparency of the points.

marker

: The marker of the points.

vmin

: The minimum value of the color map.

vmax

: The maximum value of the color map.

cmap

: The color map.

norm

: The normalization of the color map.

palette

: The color palette.

size

: The size of the points.

ax

: The axes to plot on.

rasterized

: Rasterize the points.

kwargs

: Additional keyword arguments for \_plotting.

# Examples

```
.. plot::
    :context: close-figs

    >>> import lazyslide as zs
    >>> wsi = zs.open_wsi("https://github.com/camicroscope/Distro/raw/master/images/sample.svs")
    >>> zs.pp.find_tissues(wsi)
    >>> zs.pp.tile_tissues(wsi, 256, mpp=0.5)
    >>> zs.pp.tiles_qc(wsi, scorers=["contrast"])
    >>> zs.pl.tiles(wsi, tissue_id=0, show_grid=True, color='contrast')
```

functions.name: lazyslide._plotting._viewer._get_length_string
documentation: Return a string with the length in um / mm / cm.
